questions.question: What was the reason for introducing Agile Computing(max. one phrase)?

answers.text: Challenges posed by novel and exploratory projects that involve high uncertainty, rapid change, and collaborative problem-solving.

questions.question: What are the overall goals of agility in project management?

answers.text: Adhere strictly to the initial plan

questions.question: In Agile project management, what is measured as progress?

answers.text: Readiness of a feature for review and deployment by customers

questions.question: Explain the concept of continuous improvement in Agile project management(max. one phrase)?

answers.text: Integrating customer feedback from each iteration into subsequent cycles to enhance the product over time, while adapting to changing requirements and improving features through continuous evaluation.

questions.question: What are the benefits of Agile project management compared to traditional approaches?
How does Agile project management handle change management?

answers.text: By integrating change into short cycles

questions.question: What are the primary values of the Agile Manifesto?
Describe the concept of the incremental approach in Agile project management(max. one phrase).

answers.text: Involves developing a product in increments based on feature priority, which allows for frequent delivery of usable components and facilitates adaptation to changing requirements throughout the project lifecycle.

questions.question: According to agile principles, what is the primary measure of progress in software development?
What is a core principle of agile development regarding team motivation?

answers.text: Delegating all decisions from the project leader
Encouraging team members to make decisions on their own

questions.question: What are key aspects of face-to-face communication in agile development?

answers.text: It is always conducted in a formal meeting setting
It is essential for effective information sharing among team members
It is the primary communication method regardless of circumstances
It is used alongside other communication media when necessary

questions.question: What does sustaining a constant pace of development in agile encourage?
Explain the purpose of continuous attention to technical excellence in agile development(max. one phrase).

answers.text: It ensures that the developed products meet high-quality standards, incorporate best practices, and remain adaptable to changing requirements.

questions.question: Describe the key differences between a waterfall model and an agile model in their approach to software development(max. one phrase).

answers.text: The waterfall model follows a linear, sequential approach where each phase (requirements, design, development, testing, deployment) is completed before moving to the next, with little room for revisiting or adaptating previous phases.

questions.question: What is a key limitation of the waterfall model regarding customer feedback?

answers.text: Feedback is obtained early in the process
Feedback is obtained only after testing

questions.question: How does an incremental model differ from a traditional waterfall model?

answers.text: It completes all development phases in a linear sequence
It prioritizes features into must-have, should-have, and nice-to-have categories
It releases working software only after all phases are completed

questions.question: Describe the traditional role of a project plan(max. one phrase).

answers.text: Comprehensive document that aids project managers in executing and overseeing project phases.

questions.question: Explain how an agile project plan differs from a plan used in a defined development model(max. one phrase).

answers.text: An agile project plan is viewed as a flexible guide that adapts to changing requirements.

questions.question: What does the agile iron triangle prioritize as the main fixed constraint for project success?
According to the agile iron triangle, what is the main goal of project success?
Describe the key differences between the traditional iron triangle of constraints and the agile iron triangle(max. one phrase).

answers.text: The traditional iron triangle focuses predefined scope, cost, and schedule as fixed constraints for the project success, while the agile iron triangle emphasize the ability to respond to change and deliver value to the customer, with scope and cost as variables.

questions.question: Who assumes much of the responsibility of a traditional project manager in agile projects?
What responsibilities does a Scrum Master typically have in agile projects?

answers.text: Coaching teams to achieve their goals

questions.question: Briefly describe the role of a product manager or product owner in agile projects(max. two phrase).

answers.text: The role of a product manager (or product owner) in agile projects is to ensure that the delivered product meets customer requirements.

questions.question: What is a key characteristic of T-shaped competence in agile teams?

answers.text: Narrow focus on a specific field
Ability to collaborate with specialists from other areas
Depth of knowledge not only in one area

questions.question: Name the three variables used to calculate speed according to the concept of team velocity in agile projects(max. 6 words!).

answers.text: Lead time, cycle time, response time

questions.question: How does risk response planning differ in agile projects compared to traditional projects?

answers.text: Happens at the beginning of each project phase
Involves the entire team and occurs more frequently
Is less intensive than in traditional projects

questions.question: Describe key components of an 'exception plan' in agile risk management(max. 20 words).

answers.text: Circumstances for the exception, causes of the circumstance, tolerance limits, possible solutions, recommended solutions, request for stakeholder decision.

questions.question: How is risk analysis typically performed in agile projects?

answers.text: Both quantitative and qualitative risk analysis

questions.question: What does a risk burndown chart track over time in an agile project?
What is the purpose of a spike in agile development?
How are risks monitored in agile teams?

answers.text: Recorded in a formal risk register
Monitored using highly visible information radiators
Discussed only during iteration review meetings

questions.question: Explain the role of landing zones in agile risk management(max. one phrase).

answers.text: Landing zones define a range of acceptable product functionality and characteristics agreed upon between the team and customer during planning.

questions.question: How is a short sprint called ,that usually is of fixed length and in which a team examines a solution aspect or makes a prototype to demonstrate the feasibility of this solution? (max. one word)
Who is responsible for verifying and continually improving product quality in an agile project?
What is the purpose of quality control in agile development?
Besides product quality, what are the other main areas where quality is important in agile projects?
What is technical debt in the context of agile projects?

answers.text: Financial debt incurred by the project team
Consequences of taking technical shortcuts during development

questions.question: Explain the concept of escaped defects in quality assurance.(max. one phrase)

answers.text: It refers to defects that have bypassed quality assurance processes and have been identified after the product is released or during subsequent iterations.

questions.question: Why are fixed-price contracts generally not considered suitable for agile projects?

answers.text: They allow for flexibility in scope
They align well with changing requirements
They place risk on the project team
They do not sufficiently address customer requirements

questions.question: Which types of contract operate on a pay-as-you-use basis in agile projects?
Which myths about agile project management are commonly misconceived?

answers.text: Agile project management can’t work with other plan-driven or process models
Agile project management requires no documentation
Agile project management is specific to software development
Agile projects do not require planning

questions.question: What misconceptions exist about documentation in agile project management?

answers.text: Agile teams do not create any documentation
Agile projects involve excessive planning documents
Agile projects require detailed work breakdown structures

questions.question: How should organizations approach transitioning from traditional to agile project management?

answers.text: Combine agile and traditional principles gradually
Implement agile practices without any planning

questions.question: Summarize the key points about Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI).(max. 15 words)

answers.text: CMMI provides discipline through defined activities, goals, and practices for process enhancement.

questions.question: What does the term project criticality refer to?(max. one phrase)

answers.text: Project criticality refers to the severity of the impact a project’s failure could have, on stakeholders such as customers.

questions.question: What type of organizational structure is most conducive to implementing an agile methodology?
What does the term project criticality refer to?

answers.text: The size and complexity of a project
The severity of impact a project's failure could have
The level of stakeholder involvement in a project

questions.question: What characteristics make a project best suited for an agile methodology?

answers.text: Does not require frequent reviews and adjustments

questions.question: What are some potential impacts of using an agile methodology on a project?
Why is continuous customer involvement crucial in agile methodologies?(max. one phrase)

answers.text: This ensures that requirements can evolve as work progresses and that the final product meets the customer’s needs effectively.

questions.question: When is an agile approach especially suitable for a project?

answers.text: When a project has a well-defined scope
When a project involves a high level of internal uncertainty
When a project benefits from customer feedback
When a project has a rigid and fixed timeline

questions.question: Which structural and cultural characteristics are likely to make it easier for an organization to transition to an agile methodology?

answers.text: Employees work in small teams and are accustomed to deciding how best to complete their tasks
Employees have clear roles and are given detailed work instructions
Employees do not compete with one another to achieve the best results
Employees are given only the information they need to get their work done

questions.question: Who in the Scrum team is responsible to provide a clear vision for the product to be developed?(max. 2 words)

Who in the Scrum team is responsible for ensuring that the product delivered as part of a project meets customer requirements? This role is often assumed by a local customer or a customer representative?
What do you expect from a Scrum Master?

answers.text: She/he ensures that new product features are explained to the customer clearly and in a catchy manner
She/he explains to customers the needs and constraints of the project team
She/he is responsible for tracking and monitoring project progress
She/he helps team members achieve project goals and ensure that a project is moving in the right direction

questions.question: How is the meeting called in which team members discuss what was done during the previous 24 hours, what will happen during the next 24 hours, and any obstacles that have arisen?
How is a meeting called in Scrum that takes place in large projects with multiple teams, to coordinate the work of the teams?
How is the Scrum activity called in which the team defines the tasks and goals for the upcoming iteration?(max. 2 words)
In a Scrum team, Mike is responsible for ensuring that the product delivered as part of a project meets customer requirements. What kind of  roles/responsibilities has Mike in the Scrum team?

answers.text: He is responsible for tracking and monitoring project progress
He is the main author of user stories

questions.question: In Scrum teams, there is usually one person that helps team members archive project goals and ensures that a project is moving in the right direction. Which role has this person usually in Scrum teams?
What is the name of a meeting at the end of each sprint in which the Scrum team, along with Scrum Master, Product Owner and Developers, evaluate the results of the sprint.(max. 3 words)
Who in the Scrum team measures how well a project performs in terms of return on investment, or ROI?(max. 2 words)

What is the name of the role the Scrum master adopts in order to facilitate collaboration, support everyone’s best thinking and work ,encourage the team’s participation ,undestanding ,and shared responsibility, and to change or remove organizational impediments(max. 2 words)
Which are characteristics of the optimal Scrum team?

After each sprint review meeting, the Scrum Master conducts a meeting with the development team to discuss how the sprint went and how it could have been improved. How is this meeting called?(max. 2 words)
To track its progress, a Scrum team uses which instruments?
Which of the following is a valid metric used in Scrum?
Name the five phases of the agile project management cycle in the correct order.
Identify the differences between the agile Adapting and the traditional Controlling phases.

answers.text: Adapting involves reassessing project deliverables, whereas Controlling involves working according to a pre-defined project plan
Adapting begins when the product meets all customer specifications, whereas Controlling involves planning every phase of a project
Adapting involves coordinating project work, whereas Controlling involves reassessing deliverables
Adapting begins when project objectives are determined, whereas Controlling begins when a detailed project plan is created

questions.question: What is the primary focus during the envisioning phase of an agile project?

answers.text: Determining and refining the project's vision and scope

questions.question: How does the closing phase in agile projects differ from traditional project management?

answers.text: It involves completing all project work and recording lessons learned
It only begins after several iterations with deliverables handed to the customer
It focuses primarily on handing over final deliverables to the project sponsor
It is not typically included in agile project methodologies

questions.question: What are some key differences between agile and traditional project management phases?

answers.text: Agile projects emphasize ongoing customer involvement and iterative review
Traditional projects often have a fixed, detailed project scope from the start
Agile teams are self-organizing and require extensive customer involvement
Traditional projects focus more on executing according to a predefined plan

questions.question: Which phase of an agile project involves designing, building, and testing features in increments useful to the customer?(max. 1 word)
What activities are typically conducted during the speculating phase in agile projects?

answers.text: Developing rough project and release plans
Adapting product features based on feedback

questions.question: In the adapting phase of an agile project, what are the primary responsibilities of the project leader?

answers.text: Make adjustments based on feedback and team dynamics.

questions.question: Name three important principles propagated by XP. (max. 10 words)

answers.text: Pair programming, sustainable pace, ongoing automated testing.

questions.question: Which methodology encourages no more than 40 hours of work per week to maintain a sustainable work pace?(max. 2 words)
The Adaptive Software Development (ASD) methodology consists of which of the following phases?
How is a project in the Crystal methodology called that includes up to around six members and these members are all expected to be co-located? (max. 2 words)
Which stage is part of the Dynamic Systems Development (DSDM) project life cycle?
Which methodology encourages no more than 40 hours of work per week to maintain a sustainable work pace?
How is the phase in ASD called that includes feedback from end users who have tried using the developed software? (max. 1 word)
What are the specific roles defined in a Crystal Clear project team?
What is a core principle of Lean software development?
According to Lean development, how does the principle of deliver software quickly translate into practice?

answers.text: Identifying and maintaining bottlenecks in the process
Planning smaller releases of software after shorter periods

questions.question: Briefly explain the principle of eliminating waste in Lean development.(max. one phrase)

answers.text: Removing unnecessary activities, documentation, and product features that do not add direct value to the end product.

questions.question: What is meant by sustainable pace in the context of XP?(max. one phrase)

answers.text: Practice of team members working no more than 40 hours per week to ensure consistent and reliable delivery of software

questions.question: What is a distinguishing feature of Feature-Driven Development(FDD)?

answers.text: It is unstructured but highly adaptive
It emphasizes a business perspective more than a technical one
It provides a prescriptive model that is managed at the level of individual features

questions.question: During which phase of the Unified Process(UP) does the project proceed with iterations focused on developing small, production-worthy pieces of software?
